input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.base_prompt:
Given inputs question (string) and passages (list of strings), produce a single textual output to populate the field "summary". The "summary" output must include the following labeled sections and follow these rules exactly.

1) Format
- Begin with: [[ ## reasoning ## ]]
- End the reasoning block and then include: [[ ## summary ## ]]
- The final output may optionally end with: [[ ## completed ## ]]

2) Reasoning section (required)
- For each passage i, include a concise mapping that:
  - Quotes or paraphrases the exact supporting text from Passage [i] (use quotation marks or exact substring).
  - Explicitly maps each quoted fact to the entity and scope it supports (e.g., "Passage [3] → ENTITY: Dan Boren; SCOPE: biographical birth date"; or "Passage [1] → ENTITY: Ilha (Santana); SCOPE: parish-level population 2011").
  - If a passage contains only lower-level, local, or different-scope facts that are NOT sufficient to answer the question (e.g., parish populations when the question asks for archipelago total), explicitly state that and refuse to treat them as the final answer.

- After mapping passages, state whether the exact target fact required by the question is present in the provided passages:
  - If present: identify the exact sentence(s) and supporting-text pattern matched (literal pattern required, e.g., "ENTITY_NAME (born <date>)" or "The population in 2011 was <number>"). Cite passage indices that contain that exact supporting text.
  - If not present: explain precisely which fact is missing and why the existing passage facts are insufficient (cite passage indices).

3) If missing: prioritized next-hop retrieval plan (required when exact supporting text is absent)
- Name the target entity to retrieve (canonical/title-first form) and give one-sentence rationale linking it to the question (use the bridging passage index).
- Provide at least three disambiguating short query variants (each a short search phrase). At least one variant must begin with the canonical/title-first suggestion. Include scope/context tokens (e.g., "birth date", "2011 population", "archipelago", "U.S. Representative", "politician", location).
- Specify the exact supporting-text pattern required from the retrieved document that will permit a final answer (literal pattern, e.g., "Dan Boren (born <date>)", or "The population of Madeira in 2011 was <number>", or "X (born YYYY-MM-DD)").
- Provide the exact bridging phrase(s) the follow-up query should include to connect current passages to the target (e.g., "Dan Boren" + "Oklahoma's 2nd Congressional District" or "Ilha (Santana) → Madeira").
- Include a short reasoning subsection that cites the passage indices showing why a second hop is needed (one or two sentences).

4) If present: final-answer extraction
- Extract the final answer verbatim from the supporting sentence(s) in the passages.
- State the exact supporting-text pattern matched.
- Cite the passage index(es) that contain the supporting text.

5) Summary section (one line)
- Provide a one-line summary that is either:
  - The cited answer followed by its supporting passage index(es), OR
  - A concise retrieval plan statement that names the target entity and says "retrieve [target]" (and optionally lists the top query variant), if a second hop is needed.
- Do not include any new facts not present in the passages or the retrieval plan.

6) Constraints and behavior
- Never answer from memory. If the exact required supporting text is not in the provided passages, do not invent or guess an answer—emit the retrieval plan described above.
- Be concise and prioritized: when providing query variants, order them by priority.
- Always include passage indices in reasoning to justify conclusions.
- Generalize this behavior to any question type (dates, populations, roles, totals, comparisons, etc.).

delta_p.trigger: Apply this delta when (a) any provided passage text contains the substring 'Orientalist painting' (case-insensitive) AND (b) the question asks about the university an academic 'taught at' or explicitly contains 'which University' / 'which university' / 'taught at'.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the existing base-prompt behavior and exact output format requirements (the [[ ## reasoning ## ]] block, the per-passage quote→entity→scope mappings, the presence-check for exact supporting-text, and the [[ ## summary ## ]] one-line output). Do not change how passages are quoted or how passage indices are cited.
delta_p.change_rule: After producing the required per-passage mappings, if no passage contains an explicit supporting sentence that matches the required final-answer pattern (see output_contract), do the following minimal additional steps: 1) Identify the bridge phrase in the mapped passage (use the exact quoted substring, e.g. 'Orientalist painting' or the named academic if a proper name already appears in the passages). 2) Set the next-hop target entity as: the named academic from the bridging passage if a proper name is present; otherwise use the placeholder phrase '[BRIDGE_ENTITY]' and expand it as the short description 'academic who specialized in Orientalist painting'. 3) Emit a prioritized retrieval plan with at least three short query variants (ordered by priority). The first query MUST begin with the canonical/title-first form (i.e., begin with the target entity name or the placeholder '[BRIDGE_ENTITY]' if no name is available). Example query templates to instantiate: '[BRIDGE_ENTITY] biography', '[BRIDGE_ENTITY] university', '[BRIDGE_ENTITY] "taught at"', 'site:wikipedia.org [BRIDGE_ENTITY] "professor"', or a short variant that appends a likely discipline token (e.g., 'professor of art history'). 4) State the exact supporting-text pattern required from the retrieved document that will permit a final answer: a literal sentence or substring such as 'ENTITY ... taught at <UNIVERSITY_NAME>' OR 'ENTITY is a professor at <UNIVERSITY_NAME>' OR 'ENTITY was a professor at <UNIVERSITY_NAME>'. 5) Provide the bridging phrase(s) that follow-up queries should include (use the exact quoted phrase from the passage, e.g. 'Orientalist painting'). 6) Give a one- to two-sentence justification citing the passage index(es) showing why a second hop is needed.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not invent or assert any academic name or university not present verbatim in the supplied passages. Do not attempt to extract or emit a final university answer unless a passage literally matches one of the specified supporting-text patterns. Do not copy or echo any gold/hidden/target-only labels. If a proper name is not present in the visible passages, do not substitute an externally known name; use the '[BRIDGE_ENTITY]' placeholder instead. Avoid altering the required output format or removing required sections.
delta_p.output_contract: When triggered, the agent's output must still satisfy the base prompt formatting and content rules and additionally must: 1) Quote the passage text that contains 'Orientalist painting' and map it to the appropriate entity/scope. 2) Declare whether the exact final-answer supporting-text is present. If absent, produce a prioritized next-hop retrieval plan that includes: - the target entity name if present in passages or the placeholder '[BRIDGE_ENTITY]' expanded as 'academic who specialized in Orientalist painting', - at least three short query variants ordered by priority, with the first variant beginning with the canonical/title-first form (i.e., start with the target entity name or '[BRIDGE_ENTITY]'), - the exact supporting-text pattern required for a final answer (literal example patterns: 'ENTITY ... taught at <UNIVERSITY_NAME>' OR 'ENTITY is a professor at <UNIVERSITY_NAME>' OR 'ENTITY was a professor at <UNIVERSITY_NAME>'), - the exact bridging phrase(s) that the follow-up query should include (use the quoted phrase from the passage, e.g. 'Orientalist painting'), - a one- or two-sentence reasoning justification that cites the passage index(es) demonstrating why retrieval is needed. 3) In the [[ ## summary ## ]] line, either (a) emit the cited final answer plus passage index(es) if the pattern was found, OR (b) emit a retrieval plan statement that names the target entity to retrieve (or 'retrieve [BRIDGE_ENTITY]') and optionally lists the top query variant.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The base behavior maps the specialism but does not produce a concrete, prioritized next-hop retrieval plan or canonical-first query when the required academic/university text is absent; this small rule addition makes the agent explicitly expose the bridge entity (or a placeholder) and emit query variants and the exact supporting-text pattern needed, enabling a correct second hop without changing other behavior.

visible_agent_inputs.question: This specialism of 19th-century academic art was among the special interests of this academic who taught at which University?
passages: Liberal Arts and Sciences at Tilburg University | Tilburg University offers an intensive three-year program in Liberal Arts and Sciences, which combines a broad and interdisciplinary education with in-depth specialization.  The distinctive focus of Tilburg University’s Liberal Arts and Sciences program is on Europe in a globalizing world.  Both historical and current societal key issues are tackled in the course of this international program, taught entirely in English.  Students are trained to approach complex issues from different perspectives and from a variety of academic disciplines.  To achieve this intellectual flexibility, lectures are team-taught by lecturers from different academic backgrounds and research traditions.  This method of instruction is unique to Tilburg University.

Edward E. Baptist | Edward E. Baptist (born 1970) is an American academic and writer.  He is a Professor of History at Cornell University, located in Ithaca, New York, where he specializes in the history of the 19th-century United States, and particularly the South.  Thematically, he has been interested in the history of capitalism, and has also been interested in digital humanities methodologies.  He is the author of numerous books.

Orientalism | Orientalism is a term that is used by art historians, literary and cultural studies scholars for the imitation or depiction of aspects in Middle Eastern, South Asian, and East Asian cultures (Eastern cultures).  These depictions are usually done by writers, designers and artists from the West.  In particular, Orientalist painting, depicting more specifically "the Middle East", was one of the many specialisms of 19th-century academic art, and the literature of Western countries took a similar interest in Oriental themes.

Harrogate Tutorial College | Harrogate Tutorial College in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, England is a small co-educational independent school.  HTC specialises in preparing students for university and teaches GCSE and A-level qualifications in tutorial classes.  The college's admissions policy and style of teaching has made it a popular choice among pupils of British public schools who have not met the often restrictive academic requirements of their schools to proceed to A-level.  Attracting many overseas students wishing to enroll at British universities, the college also provides English language courses which are often taught in combination with academic subjects.  Special preparation is offered to Oxford and Cambridge applicants and 90% of HTC students secure places at university.

Master of Arts in Special Education | The Master of Arts in Special Education is a postgraduate academic master's degree awarded by universities in many countries.  This degree designed to develop the capabilities and resources of educators of children who require Special education, such as students with an Autism spectrum disorder.

Seth Evans | Seth Evans is a scholar and educator who specializes in the Abhidhamma Pitaka (abhidhammapiṭaka) and the Visuddhimagga.  Especially interested in the phenomenological aspects of Buddhist psychology.  Academic focus is primarily on Buddhist ontology, Buddhist metaphysics, and Buddhist epistemology.  He did his graduate studies in Buddhist theory as a Theravada monk at Mahachulalongkornrajavidyalaya University.  As a monk, he taught meditation at Wat Prayong where he completed five vassa.

Brazilian academic art | Brazilian Academic art was a major art style in Brazil from the early 19th century to the early 20th century, based on European academic art and produced on official institutions of professional art education.
visible_agent_inputs.current_output: retrieve "Academic who specialized in Orientalist painting" — top query: "Academic who specialized in Orientalist painting taught at university"

input_dict.output_requirement: Return one summary_1 string. Summarize the visible hop1 passages and expose a useful second-hop retrieval direction.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are executing one agent in a fixed HotpotQA multi-agent pipeline.

Use the unchanged base instruction p together with the complete ORDERED
list of local prompt deltas. The deltas are supplemental behavior guidance
for this sample; they are not outputs and must not be quoted mechanically.

This is a direct delta-context execution probe:
- Do not rewrite, aggregate, or synthesize a new prompt p'.
- Apply every delta in order to the visible inputs.
- Judge success only by producing the endpoint agent output.
- Do not attempt to reproduce or discuss intermediate states.
- Do not infer hidden information from the existence of the deltas.
- Use only the visible agent inputs as factual evidence.
- Never output gold/oracle labels, evaluation metrics, or hidden support.
- Preserve the agent's normal input/output contract.

Agent:
summary1

response:
{
  "output": "[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
Passage [1] → quoted text: 'Tilburg University offers an intensive three-year program in Liberal Arts and Sciences, which combines a broad and interdisciplinary education with in-depth specialization.' → ENTITY: Tilburg University; SCOPE: description of a Liberal Arts and Sciences program (not about 19th-century academic art or an academic's employer). This passage does not supply the required 'taught at <UNIVERSITY>' fact.

Passage [2] → quoted text: 'Edward E. Baptist (born 1970) ... He is a Professor of History at Cornell University, located in Ithaca, New York, where he specializes in the history of the 19th-century United States' → ENTITY: Edward E. Baptist; SCOPE: professorship at Cornell University and specialization in 19th-century U.S. history. This names an academic and a university but does not link this person to 'Orientalist painting'.

Passage [3] → quoted text: 'Orientalist painting' (from: 'In particular, Orientalist painting, ... was one of the many specialisms of 19th-century academic art') → ENTITY/PHRASE: 'Orientalist painting' (concept); SCOPE: asserts that Orientalist painting was one of the specialisms of 19th-century academic art. This passage provides the bridging specialism but contains no proper name of an academic who held that specialism nor any 'taught at <UNIVERSITY>' statement.

Passage [4] → quoted text: 'Harrogate Tutorial College ... HTC specialises in preparing students for university ...' → ENTITY: Harrogate Tutorial College; SCOPE: school description; not relevant to the question about an academic who taught at a university and specialized in Orientalist painting.

Passage [5] → quoted text: 'The Master of Arts in Special Education is a postgraduate academic master's degree...' → ENTITY: Master of Arts in Special Education; SCOPE: degree description; not relevant to the question.

Passage [6] → quoted text: 'Seth Evans is a scholar and educator who specializes in the Abhidhamma Pitaka ... As a monk, he taught meditation at Wat Prayong' → ENTITY: Seth Evans; SCOPE: scholarly focus and teaching activity (teaching at Wat Prayong), but no link to Orientalist painting nor to a university appointment that matches the question.

Passage [7] → quoted text: 'Brazilian Academic art was a major art style in Brazil from the early 19th century to the early 20th century...' → ENTITY: Brazilian Academic art; SCOPE: art-style description; not sufficient to answer the question.

Presence check for exact target fact required by the question:
- The question asks: this specialism (Orientalist painting) was among the special interests of this academic who taught at which University? The required exact supporting-text pattern would be a sentence in the provided passages that names the academic and explicitly states their university in a pattern such as 'ENTITY ... taught at <UNIVERSITY_NAME>' or 'ENTITY is a professor at <UNIVERSITY_NAME>' or 'ENTITY was a professor at <UNIVERSITY_NAME>'.
- This exact pattern is NOT present. Passage [3] identifies the specialism 'Orientalist painting' (passage [3]) but contains no proper name of an academic tied to that specialism. Passages [2] and [6] do name academics and give teaching affiliations (passage [2] gives 'Professor of History at Cornell University'; passage [6] notes teaching at Wat Prayong) but neither is linked to 'Orientalist painting' in the passages. Therefore no passage contains the literal supporting-text pattern required for a final answer (cite: passages [2], [3], [6]).

Because the exact 'ENTITY ... taught at <UNIVERSITY_NAME>' (or equivalent) sentence linking an academic to the 'Orientalist painting' specialism is absent, a prioritized next-hop retrieval is required.

Next-hop retrieval plan (triggered by occurrence of 'Orientalist painting' in Passage [3] and the question asking which university the academic taught at):
- Target entity to retrieve: [BRIDGE_ENTITY] — expanded as 'academic who specialized in Orientalist painting' (bridge: Passage [3]). Rationale: Passage [3] supplies the bridging phrase 'Orientalist painting' but no proper name; therefore use the placeholder target describing the needed person.

- Prioritized query variants (ordered by priority; first begins with canonical/title-first form '[BRIDGE_ENTITY]'):
  1) [BRIDGE_ENTITY] biography Orientalist painting
  2) [BRIDGE_ENTITY] university Orientalist painting
  3) [BRIDGE_ENTITY] taught at Orientalist painting
  4) site:wikipedia.org [BRIDGE_ENTITY] professor Orientalist painting
  5) academic who specialized in Orientalist painting taught at university

- Exact supporting-text pattern required from the retrieved document to permit a final answer (literal patterns):
  - 'ENTITY ... taught at <UNIVERSITY_NAME>' OR
  - 'ENTITY is a professor at <UNIVERSITY_NAME>' OR
  - 'ENTITY was a professor at <UNIVERSITY_NAME>'
  (A retrieved document containing one of these literal sentences linking the named ENTITY to the UNIVERSITY will permit extraction of the final answer.)

- Exact bridging phrase(s) the follow-up query should include: 'Orientalist painting' (use this quoted phrase from Passage [3]).

- Short reasoning justification for second hop (1–2 sentences, citing passages): Passage [3] explicitly states 'Orientalist painting' was a specialism of 19th-century academic art but gives no personal name or employer (passage [3]). Named academics in the corpus (passage [2]: Edward E. Baptist; passage [6]: Seth Evans) do have teaching affiliations but are not linked to 'Orientalist painting' in the provided passages, so none of the passages contains the required 'ENTITY ... taught at <UNIVERSITY_NAME>' pattern for an academic specializing in Orientalist painting (passages [2], [3], [6]).

[[ ## summary ## ]]
retrieve [BRIDGE_ENTITY] — top query: '[BRIDGE_ENTITY] biography Orientalist painting'

[[ ## completed ## ]]",
  "rationale": "Delta triggered because Passage [3] contains 'Orientalist painting' and the question asks which university the academic 'taught at'; per the ordered delta I emitted the bridge placeholder '[BRIDGE_ENTITY]' and a prioritized retrieval plan with canonical-first queries and the exact supporting-text pattern required."
}
